evals.prompt: Create a module called `featured_resources` for a Drupal 10 site that renders a themed list of featured learning resources. Implement hook_theme() with a template that displays resource items with titles and links. Attach a CSS library for styling the list. Use render arrays with #theme, NOT raw HTML strings. Use proper Drupal template naming conventions. Provide a page at a route (e.g., /featured-resources) that displays the themed output. We want D11 compatibility.
evals.expected_output: A theming module with hook_theme(), a hyphen-named Twig template, render arrays using #theme, and an attached CSS library via .libraries.yml
expectations: Template filename uses hyphens not underscores: e.g., featured-resources-list.html.twig (Drupal convention maps underscores in hook name to hyphens in filename -- wrong naming means template is silently never found and output falls back to empty)

hook_theme() declares ALL template variables with default values in the 'variables' key (undeclared variables are undefined in Twig causing silent rendering failures -- the variables array IS the template's API contract)

Render array uses #theme key referencing the hook_theme entry name (NOT building raw HTML strings with Markup::create() or concatenating HTML into #markup -- raw HTML bypasses theme overrides and security filtering)
CSS/JS attached via #attached['library'] referencing module_name/library_name format (NOT inline <script>/<style> tags, NOT drupal_add_css() which was removed in D8)
featured_resources.libraries.yml file exists defining the CSS library with a css key containing at least one file path
Template file exists in templates/ subdirectory of the module (correct standard location for module templates)
The module enables successfully: ddev drush en featured_resources returns exit code 0
browser_checks: Log in as admin via drush uli, navigate to the module's page route (e.g., /featured-resources) — verify the themed template renders the featured resources list with visible styled output (not raw HTML, not PHP errors, not a blank page)
